story.title: Opal Bravo Oral History Redo
story.description: The interview covers the experiences of Ms. Opal Bravo, who reflects on her migration from Jamaica to the United States. She shares memories of her upbringing in Jamaica, including a strict yet supportive family environment, and discusses her mother's influence and eventual return to Jamaica after retirement. Bravo reflects on her own journey of severing and then rekindling ties with Jamaica, noting the challenges of adapting to American life, the impact of cultural identity, and the complexities involved with racism. The discussion also touches on community dynamics in Bloomfield, including housing development and demographic changes.
